extraction.one_line_summary: MILAN Yonatan won bunch sprint debut road stage after crash at 3.7km separated GC contenders inside three-kilometer rule cutoff by 700 meters.
tactical_patterns: Crash at 3.7km (700m before 3km rule) forced GC splits, punishing riders caught slightly out of position despite flat sprint stage.
LAPEIRA Paul took KOM jersey via aggressive first-climb attack at 22 years old, Grand Tour debut tactic revealing team developmental strategy.
Breakaway caught with 38km remaining triggered immediate narrowing-road tension phase, suggesting crashes predictable in this pre-finale window.

rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: Grand Tour debut road stage win shows immediate top-tier sprint power from center of road, track pedigree transferring perfectly
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Caught in 3.7km crash despite 16 stage wins of Giro experience, positioning failure on supposedly routine sprint stage
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Avoided late crash with three-kilometer safety margin alongside ROGLIC and THOMAS, GC awareness superior to crashed rivals
rider_signals.HART Tao Geoghegan: negative: Lost GC positions (4th to 8th) by being wrong side of 3.7km crash, positioning lapse costly on paper-easy stage
